name: EntityObserver
summary: This class is abstract. To implement this observer, create a Ruby class of this type, override the desired methods, and add an instance of the observer to the entity of interests. Note: The methods of this observer fire in such a way that making changes to the model while inside of them is dangerous. If you experience sudden crashes, it could be because of this observer. A potential workaround is to use a ToolsObserver to watch what the user is doing instead. This observer interface is implemented to react to entity events.

methods.name: onChangeEntity
methods.summary: The #onChangeEntity method is invoked when your entity is modified.
methods.remarks: The #onChangeEntity method is invoked when your entity is modified.

methods.name: onEraseEntity
methods.summary: The #onEraseEntity method is invoked when your entity is erased.
methods.remarks: The #onEraseEntity method is invoked when your entity is erased.
